Judgment Debtors Motion To Compel Judgment Creditor To File And Serve Acknowledgment Of Satisfaction Of Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Scott Albrecht, Esq. 949-263-0004 of Samuel Green & Steel, LLP for Petitioner.
Marshall Brubacher, Esq. 909-890-9500 of Mundell Odlum & Haws for Respondent Archipelago Lighting, Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Respondent Argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT/JUDGMENT DEBTOR ARCHIPELAGO LIGHTING, INC., DM LIGHTING TECHNOLOGIES, INC., VICTOR DENG, ARCHIPELAGO LIGHTING TEC, INC.'s Motion To Compel Judgment Creditor To File And Serve Acknowledgment Of Satisfaction Of Judgment.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Judgment Debtors' Archipelago Lighting, Inc., DM Lighting Technologies, Inc., Archipelago Lighting Tec, Inc., and Victor Deng's motion to compel judgment creditor to file and serve on judgment debtors' counsel an acknowledgement of satisfaction of judgment, or, in the alternative, for an order directing the clerk of this court to enter satisfaction of the judgment and for penalties and attorneys' fees, is denied. The record is undisputed that judgment creditor Filament Lighting, LLC did not become aware of judgment debtors' unauthorized wire transfers to judgment creditor's law firm's general operating account until March 13, 2019, five days after they were made. (Albrecht Decl., 15.) Through counsel, judgment creditor promptly rejected the second wire transfer on the ground that it was insufficient to satisfy the amount of the judgment in full. Judgment Debtors' reliance on Gray1 CPB, LLC v. SCC Acquisitions, Inc. (2015) 233 Cal.App.4th 882 is misplaced, since there the court found that "[t]he judgment was fully satisfied when [the judgment creditor] accepted the cashier's check." (Id. at 894.) Here, in contrast, Filament did not accept the second wire transfer. "Even if payment is by certified check or similar instrument, the acceptance of which arguably constitutes satisfaction [citation], the judgment creditor retains, at the least, the option of rejecting the certified check and filing the motion or memorandum for enforcement costs and fees." (Conservatorship of McQueen (2014) 59 Cal.4th 602, 615 [emphasis in original].) That is just what Filament did here. Thus, the judgment was not fully satisfied before Filament filed its March 8, 2019 memorandum of costs. (Code Civ. Proc. 685.070, 724.010(c) ["Where a money judgment is satisfied by payment to the judgment creditor by check or other form of noncash payment that is to be honored upon presentation by the judgment creditor for payment, the obligation of the judgment creditor to give or file an acknowledgement of satisfaction of judgment arises only when the check or other form of noncash payment has actually been honored upon presentation for payment."].)

The Court reserves ruling as to the amounts claimed in the March 8, 2019 memorandum of costs, which is the subject of Judgment Debtors' motion to strike set for hearing on April 26, 2019.
(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER FILAMENT LIGHTING, LLC'S Motion Order Confirming Arbitration Award And Entering Judgment In Conformity Therewith is GRANTED. No substantive opposition filed. Filament is entitled to a judgment confirming the award against DM Lighting Technologies, Inc., Archipelago Lighting, Inc., and Victor Deng aka Jia Hui Deng. There is insufficient evidence to list Jiahui H. Deng and Victor Jiahuideng as judgment debtors. The arbitration award does not list those names and Mr. Deng denies that he uses those names. Filament does not present persuasive competent evidence to contradict Mr. Deng's assertion. The declaration of Mr. Cavallo asserts that Mr. Deng uses those names based on information and belief and a review of unnamed and unprovided public records and corporate documents. This ruling is without prejudice to Filament filing a motion to amend the judgment to add the names Jiahui H. Deng and Victor Jiahuideng if the motion is supported by competent evidence. Written order is signed and filed in open court. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] judgment in accordance with the Court's ruling or file a separate request to enter judgment.
